perturbation.original_text: 1.2. The term "Business" means a branded real estate seminar style education business that uses the Licensed Intellectual Property, including a to-be-determined trademark for the Business, to market and sell customers Products through any form of communication or media.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.2. The term "Business" means a branded real estate seminar style education business that uses some of the Licensed Intellectual Property, excluding any trademark for the Business, to market and sell customers Products through any form of communication or media.
perturbation.explanation: The modification to the definition of 'Business' to exclude 'any trademark for the Business' directly contradicts the original intention where 'Licensed Intellectual Property, including a to-be-determined trademark for the Business' was included. This exclusion creates ambiguity as Section 4.1 gives LEA responsibility for 'branding the Business (including trademarks and trade dress)', suggesting trademarks are still part of the Business but are now excluded by the definition. This may affect the ownership of the 'Work Product'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4.1 LEA shall provide administrative and operational services for the conduct of the Business, including, marketing, event planning, sales, operations, information technologies, human resources, and class fulfillment. In consultation with T&B and subject to the licenses granted to LEA by T&B herein, LEA shall be responsible for branding the Business (including trademarks and trade dress) and creating and producing marketing collateral, sales presentations, course materials and other tangible work product and deliverables related to the conduct of the Business (collectively, "Work Product").
perturbation.justification: The changed text explicitly excludes 'any trademark for the Business' from the definition of 'Business.' However, the contradicted text, Section 4.1, states that LEA is responsible for 'branding the Business (including trademarks and trade dress)'. This creates a direct contradiction because trademarks are both excluded from and included in the Business, leading to uncertainty about who owns the Work Product and overall business branding. Therefore, a strong in-text contradiction exists.

perturbation.original_text: 1.12 The term "Term" shall mean an initial term of five years, automatically renewable thereafter for successive 5-year terms unless either party provides prior written notice of termination not less than 90 days prior to the end of such five-year term; provided, however, T&B shall have the right to terminate the license after the first year of the Term if LEA does not conduct the Business so as to meet the Cash Sales benchmarks set by the parties for years two through five of the Term, as set forth in Section 3.3, below.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.12 The term "Term" shall mean an initial term of five years, which will be manually renewable thereafter for successive 5-year terms upon positive assertion of both parties. The assertion of both parties must be no later than 90 days prior to the end of such five-year term; provided, however, T&B shall have the right to terminate the license after the first year of the Term if LEA does not conduct the Business so as to meet the Cash Sales benchmarks set by the parties for years two through five of the Term, as set forth in Section 3.3, below.
perturbation.explanation: The change from 'automatically renewable' to 'manually renewable thereafter for successive 5-year terms upon positive assertion of both parties' directly contradicts the original definition. The modification suggests that renewal requires explicit action from both parties, contrasting with the automatic renewal clause previously. This could create disputes about whether the contract automatically renews or requires explicit consent, especially if one party assumes automatic renewal. This in-text contradiction would also make section 11 contradictory.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 11.1 The Term shall commence upon the Effective Date and shall continue for an initial term of five (5) years. The Term shall automatically renew thereafter for successive 5-year terms unless either party provides prior written notice of termination not less than 90 days prior to the end of such five-year term.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the renewal process from 'automatically renewable' to 'manually renewable upon positive assertion of both parties.' This directly contradicts section 11.1, which states, 'The Term shall automatically renew thereafter for successive 5-year terms unless either party provides prior written notice of termination.' The original text and contradicted text both allow for automatic renewal, while the changed text requires explicit assertion by both parties for renewal, creating a clear inconsistency and contradiction within the contract.

perturbation.original_text: 4.2 LEA and T&B shall jointly own all jointly-created work product including, but not limited to, ideas, any and all concepts, designs, Customer Data (including client lists) generated through the conduct of the Business, programs, software, reports, or other intellectual property and tangible work product, produced for the Business , regardless of whether such were incorporated into or used by the Business (collectively "Work Product"), shall be and remain the joint property of LEA and T&B when produced provided, however, (i) to the extent LEA has contributed distinct and divisible work product to the Business during the Term ("LEA Work Product"), such LEA Work Product shall remain frozen for a period not to exceed 90 days, during which time T&B may acquire a license for the LEA Work Product by reimbursing LEA direct and verifiable costs LEA incurred in producing the LEA Work Product during the Term and (ii) to the extent T&B has contributed distinct and divisible work product to the Business during the Term ("T&B Work Product"), such T&B Work Product shall remain frozen for a period not to exceed 90 days, during which time LEA may acquire license for the T&B Work Product by reimbursing T&B direct and verifiable costs T&B incurred in producing the T&B Work Product during the Term. No license or right is granted hereunder at any time from LEA to T&B, or by T&B to LEA, whether expressly or by implication, estoppel or otherwise, arising out of or related to LEA Work Product or T&B Work Product, respectively. LEA shall be the owner of all LEA Work Product when created and T&B shall be the owner of all T&B Work Product when created.
perturbation.changed_text: 4.2 LEA shall own all jointly-created work product including, but not limited to, ideas, any and all concepts, designs, Customer Data (including client lists) generated through the conduct of the Business, programs, software, reports, or other intellectual property and tangible work product, produced for the Business , regardless of whether such were incorporated into or used by the Business (collectively "Work Product"), shall be and remain the sole property of LEA when produced provided, however, (i) to the extent LEA has contributed distinct and divisible work product to the Business during the Term ("LEA Work Product"), such LEA Work Product shall remain frozen for a period not to exceed 90 days, during which time T&B may acquire a license for the LEA Work Product by reimbursing LEA direct and verifiable costs LEA incurred in producing the LEA Work Product during the Term and (ii) to the extent T&B has contributed distinct and divisible work product to the Business during the Term ("T&B Work Product"), such T&B Work Product shall remain frozen for a period not to exceed 90 days, during which time LEA may acquire license for the T&B Work Product by reimbursing T&B direct and verifiable costs T&B incurred in producing the T&B Work Product during the Term. No license or right is granted hereunder at any time from LEA to T&B, or by T&B to LEA, whether expressly or by implication, estoppel or otherwise, arising out of or related to LEA Work Product or T&B Work Product, respectively. LEA shall be the owner of all LEA Work Product when created and T&B shall be the owner of all T&B Work Product when created.
perturbation.explanation: The change in Section 4.2 from 'LEA and T&B shall jointly own all jointly-created work product' to 'LEA shall own all jointly-created work product' creates an In-Text Contradiction that challenges the intellectual property arrangement. This alteration directly affects who owns the rights to the Work Product. It is further amplified by T&B continuing to be the owner of all the 'T&B Work Product'. The alteration, by establishing LEA as the sole owner of 'Work Product,' creates ambiguity on the work product from T&B
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4.2 LEA shall own all jointly-created work product including, but not limited to, ideas, any and all concepts, designs, Customer Data (including client lists) generated through the conduct of the Business, programs, software, reports, or other intellectual property and tangible work product, produced for the Business , regardless of whether such were incorporated into or used by the Business (collectively "Work Product"), shall be and remain the sole property of LEA when produced provided, however, (i) to the extent LEA has contributed distinct and divisible work product to the Business during the Term ("LEA Work Product"), such LEA Work Product shall remain frozen for a period not to exceed 90 days, during which time T&B may acquire a license for the LEA Work Product by reimbursing LEA direct and verifiable costs LEA incurred in producing the LEA Work Product during the Term and (ii) to the extent T&B has contributed distinct and divisible work product to the Business during the Term ("T&B Work Product"), such T&B Work Product shall remain frozen for a period not to exceed 90 days, during which time LEA may acquire license for the T&B Work Product by reimbursing T&B direct and verifiable costs T&B incurred in producing the T&B Work Product during the Term. No license or right is granted hereunder at any time from LEA to T&B, or by T&B to LEA, whether expressly or by implication, estoppel or otherwise, arising out of or related to LEA Work Product or T&B Work Product, respectively. LEA shall be the owner of all LEA Work Product when created and T&B shall be the owner of all T&B Work Product when created.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Section 4.2, which modifies the ownership of jointly-created work product from 'LEA and T&B shall jointly own' to 'LEA shall own,' directly contradicts the subsequent clauses within the same section. Specifically, the clauses that grant T&B ownership of 'T&B Work Product' and allow LEA to acquire a license for it by reimbursing costs. This creates a strong in-text contradiction because the initial statement that LEA owns all jointly-created work product conflicts with T&B retaining ownership of its own work product. This falls under the definition of structural flaws, since the clause is placed in multiple locations with conflicting implications. It creates uncertainty about the rights and obligations of each party. Therefore the change creates a clear and direct conflict within the document.
